About: It is about an adventure novel.

It is associated with Jean Passepartout.
It is associated with Detective Fix.
It is associated with the Reform Club.

It has a character named Aouda.
It has a character named Detective Fix.
It has a character named James Strand.

It is characterized by a wager.
It is characterized by a journey around the world.
It is characterized by mathematical precision.
It is characterized by a Scotland Yard policeman.
It is characterized by a young Indian woman.
It is characterized by a transcontinental train.
It is characterized by a steamboat.
It is characterized by a misunderstanding.
It is characterized by a deadline.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the technological innovations of the 19th century.
It was influenced by the completion of the first transcontinental railroad in America.
It was influenced by the opening of the Suez Canal.
It was influenced by the linking of the Indian railways across the sub-continent.
It was influenced by the Franco-Prussian War.
It was influenced by financial difficulties.
It was influenced by the death of a father.
It was influenced by witnessing a public execution.
It was influenced by the prospect of rapid circumnavigation.
It was influenced by the prospect of fully global tourism.
IsA: It is a work by Jules Verne.
It is a coup de théâtre.
KnownAs: It is known as Le Tour du monde en quatre-vingts jours in French.
KnownFor: It is known for being an adventure novel.
It is known for its plot.
It is known for its characters.
It is known for its wager.
It is known for its journey around the world.
It is known for its themes.
It is known for its background.
It is known for its analysis.
It is known for its technological innovations.
It is known for its historical context.
MemberOf: It is a member of the Reform Club.
OccurredAt: It occurred at the Reform Club.
It occurred at Suez.
It occurred at Bombay.
It occurred at Calcutta.
It occurred at Hong Kong.
It occurred at Shanghai.
It occurred at Yokohama.
It occurred at San Francisco.
It occurred at New York City.
It occurred at Queenstown.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Franco-Prussian War.
OccurredIn: It occurred in Egypt.
It occurred in India.
It occurred in Singapore.
It occurred in Japan.
It occurred in the United States.
It occurred in Ireland.
It occurred in England.
It occurred in France.
It occurred in Holland.
It occurred in China.
PartOf: It is part of an adventure.
PublishedAs: It was published as Le Tour du monde en quatre-vingts jours.
PublishedIn: It was published serially for the first time.
It was published in Le Temps.
UsedFor: It is used for circumnavigating the world.
It is used for a wager.
It is used for earning fare.
It is used for supporting someone.
UsedTo: It is used to travel around the world in 80 days.
It is used to attempt to circumnavigate the world.
It is used to win a wager.
It is used to complete a journey.
It is used to cover expenses.
It is used to get to Bombay early.
It is used to travel towards Calcutta.
It is used to rescue a young Indian woman.
It is used to jump bail.
It is used to earn the fare for a homeward journey.
WonBy: It was won by Phileas Fogg.
WrittenBy: It was written by Jules Verne.
